methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $690M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Bethlehem Steel Corp is a Growth-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $690M to date. The comparable universe in Robotics & Manufacturing includes 1 public company (277810.KQ) and 8 private peers, among them 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics. However, none of these comparables qualify as sufficiently narrow peers—fewer than three companies match both stage and valuation tier simultaneously.

Given the constraint on peer-based valuation, stage-progression analysis was the primary applicable method. Using total raised ($690M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of Growth-stage advancement yields $828M. This reflects the expectation that enterprise value at Growth stage should exceed cumulative capital deployed, though the multiplier remains conservative given limited precedent within the filtered peer set.

The triangulated valuation range is $704M to $952M, with a median of $828M. This range reflects a ±15% sensitivity around the single applicable method. Key uncertainties limiting confidence include the sparse comparable set at this intersection of stage and sector—the analysis relies on one method rather than cross-validation across multiple approaches. A subsequent financing event, updated unit economics, or access to same-stage robotics comps would materially strengthen conviction in this range.
